Urban Problems
⚠ Key Issues
Imbalanced land use distribution.
Low urban vitality in residential areas.
Limited green space coverage.
Traffic congestion along major corridors.
Weak pedestrian connectivity in some locations.
Development Potentials
✦ Opportunities
Strong accessibility via Galle Road.
University of Moratuwa as a development catalyst.
Potential for mixed-use development.
Opportunities to improve public spaces.
High connectivity supports economic activities.

Land Use Composition
Key Findings
Residential land use is dominant across the study area.
Commercial activities are concentrated on main roads only.
High accessibility corridors identified through Space Syntax.
Green space is below desirable urban standards.
Urban vitality varies significantly across the study area.
General Behaviour Patterns
Pedestrian activity is highest near the university.
Commercial movement follows primary roads.
Residential streets experience lower movement flow.
Peak traffic occurs at major intersections.
